---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
##library(readxl)
##library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/00-Leviathan_Cover_55.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.center[.size40[**III. How and why do non-state actors use political violence?**]]

<br>

.size50[**Today's Agenda**

- Threats of political violence tied to the 2024 election

]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Fall 2023)
]]

???

### Prep for Class
1. IDEA Evals Day

<br>




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[
.content-box-white[**IDEA Course Evaluations**]

<br>

.content-box-white[https://drury.campuslabs.com/eval-home/]
]

???





---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.content-box-purple[**For Today**]]

<br>

```{r, echo = FALSE, fig.align = 'center', out.width = '90%'}
knitr::include_graphics("libs/Images/14_3-Assignment.png")
```

???

### Everybody ready to go with this?

<br>

PRESENT each case and argument, DISCUSS and track list *ON BOARD*




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/14_3-2024_Violence_filtered.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: top, center, inverse

.textwhite[.size45[**Overall, how serious is the threat of political violence in the US in 2024?**]]

???

### Overall, how serious is the threat of political violence in the US in 2024?

- *Let this go for a bit*

<br>

### Is the primary threat of political violence in the US from the government or from non-state actors?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/14_3-Capitol_Building_v2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: top, center, inverse

.textwhite[.size45[**What are the specific vulnerabilities that make these threats possible?**]]

???

The next step for us is to use these threats to identify the vulnerabilities that make them possible.

- In other words, limiting political violence depends on our ability to find and address the vulnerabilities in our system that allow them to happen.

<br>

I am encouraging you, for this next step, to adopt a public policy lens on the problem of political violence.

- For us this will mean focusing on our domestic institutions (e.g. the rules of the game) as the sources of these vulnerabilities

<br>

Focusing on the institutions as our primary problem gives us much greater leverage on solving that problem

- In the short term, rules can be changed much more easily than people can

- In the long term, rules that become internalized as custom or habit can then transform our behavior more substantially

<br>

**SLIDE**: With this perspective in mind, let me give you the next step toward our final paper


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size60[.content-box-purple[**For Next Class**]]

<br>

.size45[
Reflect on the threats we discussed in class today in order to identify **two specific institutional vulnerabilities** that currently exist in the US. 

Describe each vulnerability and provide evidence to support your analysis.
]

???

Try to avoid any overlap!

<br>

### Questions on the assignment?
